| MCP:xklUld | # Spring Boot Template
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | A production-ready template for Spring Boot projects with modern best practices and built-in infrastructure.
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ![Docker Compose](https://imgur.com/qWLYDrg.png)
| MCP:xklUld | ![Swagger UI](https://imgur.com/5MixfZe.png)
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ## Features
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | - **Spring Boot 4.0.0** (Java 25)
| MCP:xklUld | - **UUID Primary Keys** via Hibernate `@UuidGenerator`
| MCP:xklUld | - **JWT Authentication & Authorization** with refresh tokens
| MCP:xklUld | - **Role-based Security** with Spring Security
| MCP:xklUld | - **Spring Boot Actuator** for health, info, and metrics endpoints
| MCP:xklUld | - **Request Correlation ID** — automatic `X-Correlation-ID` header propagation and MDC logging
| MCP:xklUld | - **Structured JSON Logging** — human-readable console + JSON file output (Logstash format)
| MCP:xklUld | - **Pagination** — built into `BaseService` and controllers via Spring Data `Pageable`
| MCP:xklUld | - **Rate Limiting** — annotation-based (`@RateLimit`, `@RateLimitCategory`) with configurable categories
| MCP:xklUld | - **Idempotency** — `@Idempotent` annotation with Redis-backed request deduplication
| MCP:xklUld | - **Swagger UI** for API documentation ([http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html](http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html))
| MCP:xklUld | - **PostgreSQL**, **Redis**, **Kafka & Zookeeper** — all containerized via Docker Compose
| MCP:xklUld | - **Multi-stage Dockerfile** with dependency caching and JRE runtime
| MCP:xklUld | - **JaCoCo Code Coverage** reports
| MCP:xklUld | - **Maven Enforcer** — ensures Java 25+ and Maven 3.8.7+
| MCP:xklUld | - **Lombok** and **MapStruct** for reduced boilerplate
| MCP:xklUld | - **Environment Isolation** — run dev, test, and prod in parallel via Docker Compose
| MCP:xklUld | - **Unit & Integration Tests** for services, controllers, and infrastructure
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | > **Note:** Java 25 and Maven 3.8.7+ are required.
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ---
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ## Quick Start
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ### 1. Use This Template
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | Click **"Use this template"** on GitHub, then clone your new repository:
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ```sh
| MCP:xklUld | git clone https://github.com/<your-username>/<your-repo>.git
| MCP:xklUld | cd <your-repo>
| MCP:xklUld | ```
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ### 2. Initialize Your Project
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | Run the initialization script to rename packages, modules, and containers to match your project:
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ```sh
| MCP:xklUld | ./init-project.sh --group-id com.acme --artifact my-app --name "My App" --author "Your Name"
| MCP:xklUld | ```
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | | Option | Description | Required |
| MCP:xklUld | |--------|-------------|----------|
| MCP:xklUld | | `--group-id` | Maven groupId / Java package root (e.g. `com.acme`) | Yes |
| MCP:xklUld | | `--artifact` | Artifact name in kebab-case (e.g. `my-app`) | Yes |
| MCP:xklUld | | `--name` | Human-readable project name (default: derived from artifact) | No |
| MCP:xklUld | | `--author` | Author name for `@author` tags (default: `tzesh`) | No |
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | You can also run it interactively — omit the flags and you'll be prompted:
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ```sh
| MCP:xklUld | ./init-project.sh
| MCP:xklUld | ```
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | The script will:
| MCP:xklUld | - Rename Java packages (`com.tzesh.springtemplate` -> your package)
| MCP:xklUld | - Rename module directories (`spring-boot-template-api` -> `<artifact>-api`, etc.)
| MCP:xklUld | - Update Docker container names, Kafka group IDs, and all references
| MCP:xklUld | - Replace `@author` tags if `--author` is provided
| MCP:xklUld | - Self-delete after completion
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ### 3. Verify the setup
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ```sh
| MCP:xklUld | ./mvnw clean install
| MCP:xklUld | ```
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ### 4. Start infrastructure and run
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ```sh
| MCP:xklUld | # Start PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka
| MCP:xklUld | docker compose up -d db redis zookeeper kafka
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | # Run the application
| MCP:xklUld | ./mvnw -pl spring-boot-template-api spring-boot:run
| MCP:xklUld | ```
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | Or run everything with Docker:
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ```sh
| MCP:xklUld | docker compose up -d
| MCP:xklUld | ```
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | The application will be available at [http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html](http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html).
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ---
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ## Project Structure
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ```
| MCP:xklUld | SpringBootTemplate/
| MCP:xklUld | ├── pom.xml                          # Parent POM (enforcer, JaCoCo, shared deps)
| MCP:xklUld | ├── spring-boot-template-core/       # Core module (base classes, annotations, exceptions)
| MCP:xklUld | │   └── src/main/java/.../base/
| MCP:xklUld | │       ├── annotation/              # @RateLimit, @Idempotent, etc.
| MCP:xklUld | │       ├── dto/                     # BaseDTO
| MCP:xklUld | │       ├── entity/                  # BaseEntity
| MCP:xklUld | │       ├── exception/               # NotFoundException, RateLimitExceededException, etc.
| MCP:xklUld | │       ├── handler/                 # @ControllerAdvice exception handlers
| MCP:xklUld | │       ├── mapper/                  # BaseMapper (MapStruct)
| MCP:xklUld | │       ├── response/                # BaseResponse wrapper
| MCP:xklUld | │       └── service/                 # BaseService (CRUD + pagination)
| MCP:xklUld | ├── spring-boot-template-api/        # API module (controllers, services, config)
| MCP:xklUld | │   ├── Dockerfile                   # Multi-stage build
| MCP:xklUld | │   └── src/main/java/.../
| MCP:xklUld | │       ├── config/
| MCP:xklUld | │       │   ├── correlation/         # CorrelationIdFilter
| MCP:xklUld | │       │   ├── idempotency/         # IdempotencyAspect
| MCP:xklUld | │       │   ├── ratelimit/           # RateLimitAspect, RateLimitingFilter
| MCP:xklUld | │       │   └── security/            # SecurityChain, JWT filters
| MCP:xklUld | │       ├── controller/              # REST controllers
| MCP:xklUld | │       └── service/                 # Business services
| MCP:xklUld | ├── docker-compose.yml               # PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka, app
| MCP:xklUld | ├── init-project.sh                  # Project initialization script
| MCP:xklUld | ├── .editorconfig                    # Editor formatting rules
| MCP:xklUld | ├── .gitattributes                   # Git line ending config
| MCP:xklUld | └── .dockerignore                    # Docker build exclusions
| MCP:xklUld | ```
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ---
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ## API Usage
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | 1. **Register** a new user via `POST /auth/register`
| MCP:xklUld | 2. **Login** via `POST /auth/login` to obtain a JWT token
| MCP:xklUld | 3. **Authorize** in Swagger UI using the `Authorize` button
| MCP:xklUld | 4. **Access** secured endpoints (e.g. user management requires `ADMIN` role)
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ---
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ## Actuator Endpoints
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | Health, info, and metrics are exposed at:
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | | Endpoint | Description |
| MCP:xklUld | |----------|-------------|
| MCP:xklUld | | `GET /api/v1/actuator/health` | Application health status |
| MCP:xklUld | | `GET /api/v1/actuator/info` | Application info |
| MCP:xklUld | | `GET /api/v1/actuator/metrics` | Application metrics |
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | Health details are shown when authenticated with an authorized role. Kubernetes liveness and readiness probes are enabled.
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ---
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ## Correlation ID
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | Every request is assigned a correlation ID for distributed tracing:
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | - If the client sends an `X-Correlation-ID` header, it is reused
| MCP:xklUld | - Otherwise, a UUID is generated automatically
| MCP:xklUld | - The correlation ID is added to the MDC for all log output and returned in the response header
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ---
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ## Rate Limiting
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ### Annotation-based (per-endpoint)
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ```java
| MCP:xklUld | @RateLimit(limit = 20, duration = 1, key = RateLimitKeyStrategy.USER)
| MCP:xklUld | @RateLimitCategory(RateLimitCategoryType.STANDARD)
| MCP:xklUld | ```
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ### Configurable categories in `application.properties`
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | | Category | Default Limit | Duration |
| MCP:xklUld | |----------|--------------|----------|
| MCP:xklUld | | `strict` | 10 req | 1 min |
| MCP:xklUld | | `standard` | 60 req | 1 min |
| MCP:xklUld | | `relaxed` | 200 req | 1 min |
| MCP:xklUld | | `authentication` | 5 req | 1 min |
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ---
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ## Idempotency
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | Annotate mutating endpoints with `@Idempotent` to prevent duplicate processing. Clients must send an `Idempotency-Key` header. Backed by Redis.
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ```java
| MCP:xklUld | @PostMapping("/")
| MCP:xklUld | @Idempotent
| MCP:xklUld | public ResponseEntity<BaseResponse<UserDTO>> createUser(...) { ... }
| MCP:xklUld | ```
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ---
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ## Kafka Messaging
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | - Kafka and Zookeeper are included in Docker Compose
| MCP:xklUld | - `POST /kafka/send` sends messages to Kafka (supports optional scheduled delivery)
| MCP:xklUld | - Consumer logs received messages
| MCP:xklUld | - Logging level configurable in `application.properties`
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ---
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ## Environment Configuration
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | Use `.env` files for environment-specific variables and Docker Compose override files for port/resource separation:
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ```sh
| MCP:xklUld | # Run test environment
| MCP:xklUld | docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.test.override.yml --env-file .env.test -p myapp_test up -d
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | # Run prod environment
| MCP:xklUld | docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.prod.override.yml --env-file .env.prod -p myapp_prod up -d
| MCP:xklUld | ```
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ---
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ## Code Coverage
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | JaCoCo reports are generated during `mvn test` and available at:
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ```
| MCP:xklUld | spring-boot-template-api/target/site/jacoco/index.html
| MCP:xklUld | spring-boot-template-core/target/site/jacoco/index.html
| MCP:xklUld | ```
| MCP:xklUld | 
| MCP:xklUld | ---
| MCP:xklUld |

Now I have a complete picture. Here's the comprehensive answer:

---

## Environment-Specific Execution in This Template

### Infrastructure Services Started with Docker Compose

The `docker-compose.yml` defines **5 containerized services** on a shared bridge network:

| Service | Image | Purpose |
|---------|-------|---------|
| **db** | `postgres:16` | PostgreSQL database (port 5432) |
| **redis** | `redis:7` | Redis for rate limiting, idempotency, and session storage (port 6379) |
| **zookeeper** | `confluentinc/cp-zookeeper:7.5.0` | Kafka coordination (port 2181) |
| **kafka** | `confluentinc/cp-kafka:7.5.0` | Message broker (port 9092), depends on zookeeper |
| **app** | Built from `spring-boot-template-api/Dockerfile` | Spring Boot application (port 8080), depends on db and redis |

The `app` service is a multi-stage Docker build: a JDK 25 builder stage compiles the code, then copies the JAR into a slim JRE 25 runtime image with a built-in `/actuator/health` healthcheck.

### How Test and Prod Environments Are Distinguished

The template uses a **three-layer isolation strategy**:

#### 1. `.env` Files (Environment Variables)

Each environment has its own env file with distinct database names, users, and Spring profiles:

| File | `SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE` | `POSTGRES_DB` | `POSTGRES_USER` |
|------|--------------------------|---------------|-----------------|
| `.env` / `.env.dev` | `dev` | `spring_template_dev` | `development` |
| `.env.test` | `test` | `spring_template_test` | `test` |
| `.env.qa` | `qa` | `spring_template_qa` | `qa` |
| `.env.prod` | `prod` | `spring_template_prod` | `production` |

All environments share the same password placeholder (`T3st!ng`); in practice you'd use real secrets.

#### 2. Spring Profile Properties (Application Behavior)

Each profile has its own `application-{profile}.properties` under `src/main/resources/`:

| Profile | `server.port` | `logging.level.root` |
|---------|---------------|----------------------|
| `dev`   | 8080 | **DEBUG** |
| `test`  | 8081 | INFO |
| `qa`    | 8082 | INFO |
| `prod`  | 8083 | **WARN** |

All profiles inherit shared configuration from `application.properties` (server context path, JPA, JWT, Redis, Kafka, rate-limiting, actuator settings). The profile files layer on top, overriding only the port, logging level, and database driver/dialect.

#### 3. Docker Compose Override Files + Project Names (Parallel Execution)

The README documents that environments can run **simultaneously** using override files and Docker Compose project names:

```sh
# Test environment
docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.test.override.yml \
  --env-file .env.test -p myapp_test up -d

# Prod environment  
docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.prod.override.yml \
  --env-file .env.prod -p myapp_prod up -d
```

The `-p` (project name) flag prefixes all container names so test and prod containers don't collide. The override files (e.g., `docker-compose.test.override.yml`, `docker-compose.prod.override.yml`) are intended to remap host ports so each environment's PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka, etc. can coexist on the same host. Note: these override files are **referenced in the README but not committed to the repository**—users are expected to create them for their own port-mapping needs.

### Summary

The documentation describes a layered approach: **`.env` files** select the Spring profile and isolate the database per environment; **profile-specific `application-{env}.properties`** tune runtime behavior (ports, logging verbosity); and **Docker Compose override files with project names** allow multiple full stacks to run side-by-side. Infrastructure is fully containerized (PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka + Zookeeper), and the app itself uses a multi-stage Dockerfile with JRE runtime and a healthcheck probe.
